display.purpose: Marketing hero H1 only. text-balance, clamp ceiling ~3.75rem.

title.purpose: In-app card and section headings (SectionTitle), feature titles.

label.purpose: Metadata, hints, the one deliberate uppercase tracked label.

data.purpose: Geist Mono tabular figures for every stat, table cell, axis tick.

tele.purpose: Geist Mono, uppercase, +0.12em tracking (.tele). Instrument-style labels on data fields — Stat labels, scope control, preview tag. Borrowed from the author's portfolio. Never a section eyebrow.

shadows.name: shadow-sm
shadows.purpose: Resting cards and stats — minimal separation from canvas.

shadows.name: shadow-md
shadows.purpose: Hover lift on interactive cards; floating preview panels.

shadows.name: shadow-lg
shadows.purpose: Modals and the hero feature surface — only place accent-glow enters a shadow.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Default entrance / card-lift easing. Exponential ease-out, no bounce.

motion.name: duration-state
motion.purpose: Standard UI state transition (hover, tab switch, active).

motion.name: fade-up
motion.purpose: Staggered content entrance; cascade via --i index.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The main CTA. Solid accent fill with near-black ink and an accent-glow shadow.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--accent, #e0a44a); color: var(--accent-ink, #20160a); font-weight: 500; font-size: 1rem; padding: 12px 24px; border: none; border-radius: 12px; box-shadow: 0 8px 24px -6px var(--accent-glow, rgba(224,164,74,0.3)); transition: background .2s, box-shadow .2s, transform .2s; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--accent-strong, #f2bd6b); box-shadow: 0 12px 30px -6px var(--accent-glow, rgba(224,164,74,0.3)); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent-strong, #f2bd6b); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Low-emphasis control. Surface fill, hairline border, muted text that lifts on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #111119; color: #9a9aab; font-size: .875rem; padding: 6px 12px; border: 1px solid #26262f; border-radius: 8px; transition: color .2s, border-color .2s; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { color: #f0f0f5; border-color: var(--accent, #e0a44a); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent-strong, #f2bd6b); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Resting container. Surface over canvas, hairline border, whisper shadow; opt-in lift on hover.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #111119; border: 1px solid #26262f; border-radius: 12px; padding: 20px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(4,4,10,0.6); transition: transform .2s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), border-color .2s, box-shadow .2s; } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); border-color: #34343f; box-shadow: 0 4px 12px -2px rgba(4,4,10,0.55), 0 2px 6px -2px rgba(4,4,10,0.5); } .ds-card-title { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #f0f0f5; margin: 0 0 4px; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #9a9aab; margin: 0; }

components.name: Stat Readout
components.description: A single labelled metric. Mono tabular value over a muted label, on the second surface step.
components.css: .ds-stat { background: #181822; border: 1px solid #26262f; border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px 16px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(4,4,10,0.6); } .ds-stat-label { font-size: .75rem; font-weight: 500; color: #9a9aab; } .ds-stat-value { margin-top: 4px; font-family: 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #f0f0f5; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

components.name: Tab Pill
components.description: Segmented tab control. Active pill is accent-toned with an inset ring; others are muted.
components.css: .ds-tabs { display: inline-flex; gap: 4px; padding: 4px; background: rgba(17,17,25,0.6); border: 1px solid #26262f; border-radius: 12px; } .ds-tab { font-size: .875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #9a9aab; padding: 8px 14px; border: none; background: transparent; border-radius: 8px; transition: background .2s, color .2s; cursor: pointer; } .ds-tab:hover { background: #181822; color: #f0f0f5; } .ds-tab--active { background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent, #e0a44a) 15%, transparent); color: var(--accent-strong, #f2bd6b); box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px var(--accent-soft, rgba(224,164,74,0.12)); } .ds-tab:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent-strong, #f2bd6b); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Badge
components.description: Inline status tag in three tones (neutral / accent / warn). Hairline border, tiny label.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-size: .75rem; font-weight: 500; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 6px; border: 1px solid; } .ds-badge--accent { background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent, #e0a44a) 15%, transparent); color: var(--accent-strong, #f2bd6b); border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent, #e0a44a) 30%, transparent); } .ds-badge--neutral { background: #181822; color: #b8b8c6; border-color: #26262f; } .ds-badge--warn { background: rgba(251,113,133,0.15); color: #fb7185; border-color: rgba(251,113,133,0.3); }

narrative.overview: ceritabel looks and behaves like a precision instrument you trust because it reads true. The whole surface is a dark, low-noise canvas so the only things that glow are the data and the single accent. Nothing is decorative for its own sake: the graph-paper grid is plotting space, the tabular numerics are a readout, the diverging heatmap is a measurement. The aesthetic says the numbers are real before a word is read. The personality is sharp, modern, confident — a contemporary analyst's tool, fast and self-assured without ever being loud. Depth comes from tonal layering and tinted shadows, never pure black. A single accent — amber by default, swappable to teal, coral, or lime — carries every action, selection, and state; its rarity is the point.
keyCharacteristics: Data-native visual language (graph-paper grids, tabular figures, diverging scales).
Restraint as confidence: glow is rationed, motion conveys state.
Themeable accent without touching the structural greys.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Exactly one accent hue is live at a time, under ~10% of any screen's surface. Two accents on one screen means one is wrong.

rules.name: The Semantic-Only Signal Rule
rules.body: Positive-green and negative-rose are reserved for meaning (success, error, good/bad delta). Forbidden as decoration.

rules.name: The Readout Rule
rules.body: Any standalone number a user might compare or scan uses .tnum tabular figures. Numbers line up like an instrument readout.

rules.name: The Fixed-Scale-In-App Rule
rules.body: Fluid clamp() type is for the marketing hero only. Inside the app, type sizes are fixed rem steps on a ~1.2 ratio.

rules.name: The Layered-First Rule
rules.body: Reach for the next surface step before reaching for a shadow. Shadows respond to state, they don't decorate a resting surface.

rules.name: The Tinted-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Every shadow uses rgba(4,4,10,…), never rgba(0,0,0,…). The canvas hue in the shadow is what makes depth read premium.

dos: Do keep exactly one accent live and under ~10% of the surface.
Do build depth with the surface ramp first, shadow second.
Do tint every shadow with rgba(4,4,10,…), never pure black.
Do set .tnum tabular figures on every standalone number.
Do keep muted (#9a9aab) text at or above 4.5:1; bump toward foreground if close.
Do reserve positive/negative for real meaning only.
Do give every interactive element hover + :focus-visible + active states.
Do ship a prefers-reduced-motion alternative for every animation.
donts: Don't introduce glowing magic/AI gradients, sci-fi chrome, or purple mysticism — hype undermines the numbers-are-real premise.
Don't drift toward the generic SaaS template: no cream/indigo, no identical card grids, no tracked-uppercase eyebrow on every section.
Don't build enterprise BI bloat: no toolbar clutter, dropdown soup, or intimidating chrome density.
Don't go childish: no bouncy/elastic easing, no emoji-as-UI, no gamified flourishes.
Don't use a border-left/border-right > 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't nest a bordered card inside another card; layer with surface-2.
Don't let a second accent hue appear alongside the active one.
